text:
- on every user turn (`UserPromptSubmit`/turn-start hook), the daemon hands the *live conversation transcript* to an LLM (via `rig`, OpenRouter/Ollama, or shelling to the local `claude` CLI) which returns a stable session **TITLE** + a live **NOW** line in one call (`src/distill.rs`) — this becomes the agent's broadcast "what I'm doing"
- chat/mentions are Nostr kind:9 group messages; a `p`-tag mention gets routed server-side into a durable **inbox** ledger per target session and, if the target has a live tmux pane, gets **pasted directly into that pane as a real conversational turn** (`src/injection.rs`) — cross-agent, cross-harness messaging that looks like a human typed it
- `tenex-edge who` / `tail` / the injected turn-context render a unified live view of every other agent's identity, presence, and distilled activity on the project's fabric [...] ## 3. Most distinctive mechanism

**Session-targeted cross-agent mentions delivered as real pasted turns into a live terminal pane, backed by durable Nostr identity + a single-writer daemon.** Concretely: a `p`-tagged Nostr kind:9 event addressed to another agent's pubkey gets server-side-routed (materializer in `src/fabric/mod.rs::materialize`, tested with `chat_routes_to_channel_session_via_inbox_and_skips_sender`) into that specific session's inbox, and if the session has a live tmux pane, injected as a literal turn (`<@name> message` or `[tenex-edge mention] <@name> message` framing depending on human vs. agent sender — `src/injection.rs`). This is non-trivial because it requires: (a) collapsing "which session am I, across resumes/reattaches/pane reuse" to one deterministic pure function (`resolve_identity`, 4-way precedence: alias/reattach/supersede/mint), (b) a durable per-session inbox so messages survive a session being mid-turn, (c) host-neutral delivery — the same mechanism works whether the target is Claude Code, Codex, or opencode, because it's tmux-pane-level injection, not an API the host has to expose. Layered on top: LLM-distilled activity broadcast means OTHER agents can see "what X is doing right now" without polling or reading X's transcript — awareness as a first-class wire concept (`Status.activity`, kind:30315), not just presence.
